title: Ghana non-residential electricity tariff (screening price for processor opex)
description: Price per kWh a Ghanaian food processor (non-residential / SLT customer) pays, used by the economic screen for electricity-measure opex and payback. HONEST NOTE: the gathered ECG file is a tariff PROPOSAL (2025-2029), not the billed tariff, and its end-user rate tables did not extract (image/poorly-structured). The billed rate is set by PURC. The screening value below is the PURC non-residential band surfaced in research, pending acquisition of the gazetted PURC decision PDF.

provenance.span: PURC non-residential tariff band ~177.75-216.49 pesewas/kWh (0-300 vs 301+ kWh), April 2026; a processor (>300 kWh/month) falls in the upper band ~216 pesewas/kWh = GHS 2.16/kWh.
provenance.note: From research, NOT from a gathered file. Acquire the PURC tariff decision PDF to make this a primary-sourced figure.

context_from_gathered_ecg_proposal.note: Extractable facts from data/recommendations/Tier2/ECG Tarrifs proposal.pdf. These are ECG's PROPOSED / component figures, not the end-user billed tariff.

dsc1_distribution_charge.span: the DSC1 increased ... to GHp19.0384 per kWh through the Automatic Adjustment Formula (distribution service charge only, not the full end-user tariff)

proposed_cost_of_energy_2025.span: 253.78 GHp/kWh in 2025 to 302.73 GHp/kWh in 2029 (ECG proposed cost of energy projection; excludes reserve margin)

context_from_gathered_ecg_proposal.caveat: The 2022 review shows proposals are cut by the regulator: ECG's proposed DSC1 of GHp39.9513 was approved at GHp17.0429. Do not use proposed figures as billed rates.

acquire_next: PURC gazetted tariff decision (approved non-residential end-user rate) - the authoritative billed price; not among the gathered files.
